# Questions in Livestream 264

Live Stream #264: The Shasu of Vancouver

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=1476" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:36</a> | Is it better to wait for all the books in a series or read them as they come out? | He advised reading them as they release, especially while the series is still actively being written. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=1714" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:34</a> | If one could choose only one vocation, should it be artist, writer, painter, musician, or scholarship? | He said the practical question is what kind of poverty one is willing to accept, then cautioned that scholarship is not usually a good way to earn a living unless chosen carefully. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=1913" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:53</a> | What are his thoughts on grilled cheese sandwiches? | He likes them and suggested combinations such as brie with fruit and balsamic or honey, while emphasizing good melting cheese. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=1973" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:53</a> | Butter or mayo for grilled cheese? | He said both can work depending on what one is doing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=2136" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:36</a> | What book would he recommend on Gnosticism? | He recommended starting with his own book on ancient Egypt and the origins of Gnosticism, with the caveat that the best entry point depends on prior background. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=2268" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:48</a> | Is Ezekiel 38-39 about modern Israel? | He said it concerns a future Israel, but because apocalyptic literature is often reinterpreted for later contexts, the text does not let him identify it confidently with present-day Israel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=2383" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:43</a> | Are there different levels of hell? | He said the Bible is silent on that; later literature such as Dante influenced the idea, but there is no clear biblical warrant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=2607" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:27</a> | Is physical media for video games and media in general dead or dying? | No. He said physical media is seeing a revival as buyers react to digital purchases being removed or controlled by platforms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=2766" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:06</a> | Will Archive.org remain available in light of recent lawsuits? | He thought it likely survives, though he noted legal pressure on multiple archival platforms and some uncertainty about outcomes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=2890" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:10</a> | What caused the programming language assembly to become extinct? | He said assembly is difficult, verbose, poor for large modular projects, and now mostly limited to boot code. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=2950" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:10</a> | What caused the programming language Perl to become extinct? | He said Perl is difficult to debug, and Python displaced it because programmers could spend more effort coding and less effort debugging. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=3065" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:05</a> | What are his thoughts on point-and-click adventure games? | He said they have their place and there is nothing wrong with them, though they are not generally his preference. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=3115" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:55</a> | What video-editing program does he use, and why? | He uses Adobe Premiere Pro largely because it is the tool he has used since college and already knows well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=3148" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:28</a> | What storage platform does he use, and why? | He avoids cloud storage, disables it when possible, and prefers local/NAS storage because of privacy, control, and terms-of-service concerns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=3267" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:27</a> | What does he do when he has to change computers? | He uses a NAS and copies data to or from it when moving between machines. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=3330" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:30</a> | Is a NAS like a flash drive? | No. He described it as network-attached storage, essentially a small mirrored storage array accessible over the network. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=3538" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:58</a> | Does the destruction date of Hazor seem suspicious because of two Philistine sherds associated with Moshe Dothan? | He said not necessarily; archaeology is corroborative, and later work at Hazor matters more than attacking a result because of the excavator. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=3716" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:56</a> | Could Joseph have risen to power in Egypt even without Hyksos rule? | Yes. He said Egypt sometimes elevated Asiatic Semites to very high office even outside the Hyksos period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=4140" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:00</a> | Are there any updates on the so-called curse amulet? | He said the topic is effectively dead and no longer looks viable as a serious line of evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=4802" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:02</a> | Has he known PhDs who were incompetent in their own field or outside their expertise? | Yes. He said he has met scholars who were badly out of their depth despite holding the credential. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=5245" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:25</a> | What exactly is Proverbs 17:8 saying about bribes? | He said Proverbs are often descriptive maxims, and this one observes that bribes open doors without endorsing bribery as good or fair. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=5530" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:10</a> | Can he give a brief summary of Egyptian prehistory up to the Old Kingdom? | He said it is too complex to compress neatly, then sketched the long developmental sequence rather than pretending there is a simple one-line summary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=6506" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:26</a> | Why accept Noonan's argument for Egyptian composition of Exodus but not linguistic arguments for multiple authorship of Isaiah? | He said Egyptian loanwords in Exodus are clearer and more datable, while Isaiah authorship arguments involve more subjective linguistic judgments and have counterarguments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=6617" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:17</a> | From a Christian perspective, would it be acceptable if a biblical book had multiple authors? | Yes. He said multiple inspired authors would still be compatible with Christian belief. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=6650" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:50</a> | Could the school of Isaiah have contained a second Isaiah? | He said it is possible, but he thinks single authorship is more likely. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=6862" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:22</a> | In Exodus 6:20, was the later prohibition on such unions a correction of a strange custom, with Moses ironically born from such a union? | He said the relationship language is ambiguous and may reflect clan or kinship terminology, so it is not clear the later prohibited direct relationship is actually what is happening. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=7276" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:16</a> | What is his favorite published resource from Kenneth Kitchen? | He chose Kitchen's Ramesside Inscriptions, especially because it is a core resource for Egyptologists. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=7372" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:52</a> | How close is modern Hebrew to biblical Hebrew? | He said modern Hebrew is not the same language form, but a simplified descendant with reduced syntax and other streamlining. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=7510" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:10</a> | Has he heard of the butterfly people reported during the 2011 Joplin EF5 tornado, and does he think they were angels or another kind of spirit? | He said he did not know the incident and therefore could not really comment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=7606" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:46</a> | Does he think Jesus was crucified in AD 30 or AD 33? | He leans toward AD 30, though he said the difference does not matter much to him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=7636" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:16</a> | What is his opinion on manga, comics, or graphic novels compared with prose novels? | He said they are different forms with different strengths; he enjoys manga and graphic novels but has lately read more prose to work on attention span. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=7894" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:34</a> | Does the obelisk represent Osiris's missing phallus? | No. He said obelisks relate to sun cults, not Osiris. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=7940" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:20</a> | What is his opinion on data centers? | He said they are cold, uncomfortable, dusty, and that he disliked working in them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=8012" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:32</a> | Which is more useful for the edification of the church, artists or scholarship? | He said both are needed: scholarship supplies the data, while the arts communicate it to the culture and to ordinary people. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=8205" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:45</a> | Is it equally important to have the paleontologist and the paleo artist? | Yes. He compared it to the relationship between doctors and medical illustrators: data needs careful presentation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=8348" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:08</a> | Has he considered Pope Leo XIV's Magnifica Humanitus, especially on AI and just war? | He had not looked at the just war material, found the AI concerns somewhat alarmist, and argued for a middle position on AI use. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=8557" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:37</a> | What does he think of identifying Mary the mother of Jesus with the Ark of the Covenant? | He rejected the claim as bad apologetics and said it lacks support in the church fathers and the text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=8796" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:36</a> | What is his opinion of Josephus's writings about Jesus? | He said Josephus is mostly dispassionate, with one possible interpolation, and that removing it would not change the basic historical value of the passage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=9132" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:12</a> | Is the alleged Q source legitimate, and did it exist? | He said we do not know, and he argued that there is no direct evidence for Q. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=9262" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:22</a> | What are his thoughts on aliens and their relationship to demons? | He said it depends on the case: some claims may be optical illusions, some alleged psychic contact could be demonic, and some abduction stories may have other explanations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=9849" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:09</a> | Does disagreement about the date of the Exodus undermine someone's Christian faith? | He rejected that framing and said he is traditional on essentials of the faith even though others have treated his Exodus dating position suspiciously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=10009" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:49</a> | What is his opinion on Albert Mohler's amendment to prevent Southern Baptist Convention churches from having female pastors? | He had not read the amendment and took a middle position: male pastors should be the general rule for congregations, but some specialized ministries require women. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=10187" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:47</a> | Are there published 3D animations that look indistinguishable from 2D? | He said 3D can be made to look 2D because the final image is already a two-dimensional presentation, and much modern 2D animation uses 3D models. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=10761" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:21</a> | What is the synagogue of Satan? | He said it refers to those who actively conspire against Christ and persecute Christians, not ordinary Jewish congregations as such. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=10982" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:02</a> | Was Akhenaten a monotheist, a henotheist, or something else? | He described Akhenaten as a monolatrous pantheist rather than a true monotheist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=11210" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:50</a> | Does he delete caches on his web browser, and if so, how? | Not normally; he said he sets the browser to automatic purge and otherwise leaves it alone unless space is short. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=11353" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:13</a> | Does he mean writers should use exposition dumps of information in fiction? | No. He tries to avoid info dumps and gives readers only enough information to orient them, weaving more in naturally later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=12194" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:14</a> | With all Israel's complaints and rebellion in Numbers, would other ancient Near Eastern gods have rejected and abandoned Israel in the wilderness? | He said probably not, because Levantine idolatry often imagined gods as being bound or manipulated through idols rather than relating like the God of Israel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=12416" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:26:56</a> | Should AI be considered an attempt to usurp God's role as creator of life, and therefore blasphemy? | He said AI cannot create life; people may idolize it, but that does not make AI itself an act of creating life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=13188" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:39:48</a> | What is the difference between soul and spirit? | He said the distinction is difficult and may be small or nonexistent in a bipartite view, though he later allowed there might be a hard-to-define difference. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=13722" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:48:42</a> | Is there a difference between a mind and a brain? | He said the mind is more or less the unity of soul and spirit, rather than simply the physical brain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=14100" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:00</a> | Would mosasaurs have been intelligent in the same way as monitor lizards? | Yes. He said they would likely have similar intelligence based on reptile brain structure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=14159" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:59</a> | Is the separation of mind and brain a theory? | He said it is a theory some hold, but he tends to see the mind as a function of the brain and takes an emergent-dualism position. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=14360" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:59:20</a> | How long was the journey from Mount Sinai to Paran, and would Numbers have been short if the first generation obeyed? | He said the journey could have been only a few days for a small caravan, so Numbers would have been much shorter if Israel had obeyed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=14532" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:02:12</a> | Do most denominations with female pastors or priests become theologically liberal? | He said most of them do, in his view, and treated that as a recurring pattern rather than an exception. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=14816" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:06:56</a> | How accurate is his history? | The subject is unclear in the transcript, but he answered "middling," saying some things are wrong and many things are right. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=14856" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:07:36</a> | Why does he not like Simon the Magician? | He said Simon Magnus tried to use the Holy Spirit for profit, which is where the term simony comes from. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=14954" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:09:14</a> | How religious were ancient Israelites before being scattered to Assyria and Babylon? | He said they were extremely religious, often worshiping many gods alongside Yahweh, which was precisely the problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=15147" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:12:27</a> | Who does he think the younger lady from KV35 is, and why is her mouth the way it is? | He said the mouth is likely a post-mortem effect, not evidence of a horrible death, and that genetic evidence identifies her as King Tut's mother though her name is unknown. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=15323" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:15:23</a> | What is gopher wood? | He said we do not know; speculative guesses lack evidence and comparative ancient Near Eastern material does not clarify it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=15370" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:16:10</a> | How are the bunny and shrimp? | He said the bunny was recovering from an eye infection and the shrimp were doing fine. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=15434" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:17:14</a> | Why is the suicide rate in seminaries higher than average? | He attributed it to the difficulty and stress of seminary, isolation, and the way some students' study becomes detached from worship and faith. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=15806" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:23:26</a> | Does a speech impediment automatically disqualify someone from being an evangelist? | No. He said it may make some things harder, but it can also make someone a better listener and is not a disqualifier. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=15904" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:25:04</a> | What occupation options are there after finishing seminary education? | He said many seminary graduates never become pastors and instead move into sales, corporate leadership, strategy, organization, or other roles using transferable skills. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/vcMbdIAWPX0?t=16321" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:32:01</a> | How did he make his speech impediment go away? | He said it did not go away; he still has it and manages it consciously. |
